15. General operation
15.1 The Panda iControl2 panel
The "Panda iControl2 panel" control panel is the control and display unit for the Panda iControl2 control system and
represents the interface between the user and the Panda iControl2 controller. The integrated display serves to
present the most important data of the system as well as warnings and error messages. 
The control panel is equipped with four buttons for operating the Panda iControl2 controller:
Fig. 15.1-1: Panda iControl 2 panel
1. On/Off button: Switching the Panda iControl2 controller on and off
2. Start/Stop button: Starting and stopping the generator, confirming values in selection menus (Enter key)
3. Cursor-up button Switching between display screens (up), counting values up in selection menus
4. Cursor-down button Switching between display screens (down), counting values down in selection menus.

brightness) and "backlight 2" (dimmed brightness). These service menu screens are highlighted with the light bulb
symbol:

In this screen, you can enter the time in seconds, values between 0s and 255s are

15.3.3 Operating modes
The Panda iControl2 controller offers different operating modes.
15.3.3.1  Stand-by mode
After the controller is switched on with the On/Off button, the system is in stand-by mode. This is indicated by the
output "STANDBY" in the status field in the top right corner of the default display screen. In this operating mode, the
system can be switched off with the On/Off button, and the generator can be started up with the Start/Stop button.
With the cursor buttons, the service information screen can be accessed.

18.2 Faults
Error messages are issued when the monitored variable, e.g. a temperature, reaches the defined fault threshold. 
With the temperature sensors, a loose connection or a broken cable will result in a fault, as well, and cause the
generator to shut down. 
An error message is typically preceded by a warning, as the warning threshold is reached before the fault threshold.
Error messages are output on the Panda iControl2 panel display in the form of the error text shown on a cleared
display page. Faults do not become active until the time between reaching the fault threshold and the defined delay
has expired.
Faults result in the generator shutting down. If a fault occurs due to the battery voltage being too low, the controller is
completely shut down to prevent the battery from discharging too much.

18. Warnings and error messages
To enable the safe operation of the generator, the Panda iControl2 controller is programmed with a series of
warnings and error messages that influence the generator operation. 
18.1 Warnings
Warnings are issued when the variable being monitored, e.g. temperature, reaches a defined warning threshold.
The warnings are issued on the Panda iControl2 panel display via the cyclical display of the word „HIGH“ or „LOW“,
alternating with the measured value, e.g. the temperature. Warnings do not become active until the time between
reaching the threshold value and the defined delay has expired.
Note!Warnings do not result in the generator or the controller 
being switched off.
18.1.1 Examples of warnings on the display:
Fig. 18.1.1-1: Warning: „Battery power too low“Warning: „Battery power too low“
Fig. 18.1.1-2: Warning: „Winding temperature too high“Warning: „Winding temperature too high“

18.3 The error memory of the iControl 2 Panel
From Software version PiC2_2.9 (control board) and PiP2_2.9 (control panel) the Panda iControl2 has got an error
memory which shows the last six errors in the plain text.
18.3.1 How to get to the error memory of the iControl2 Panel?
The error memory is easily accessible via the setup menu of the control panel which is open to every user.
The setup menu can be accessed as usual:
• To access the setup menu, please press the key "Cursor Down" directly after switching on the control while the 
panda bear is displayed.
• Now you can see the setup menu and its menu items.

• To display the error memory please select the menu item Error mem.
18.3.2 How are stored errors displayed?
The errors are displayed in the plain text. The error is preceded by the operating hour when the error occurred. The
fault having the highest operating hour will be displayed in the first row. Older error entries are displayed in the rows
below in descending order of the operation hour. If the memory contains already six errors, the oldest entry is
deleted.
Expample for displaying an error entry: 3045.2h COMMUNICATION
This means: In operating hour 3045.2 an error in the bus communication has occurred.
18.3.3 How do I exit the error memory after having read the entries?
You can return to the standby page via the start-stop-key.

• Clean engine as per manufacturer's instructions. 
• Spray engine parts and V-belt disks with preservative. 
• Clean air filter housing and spray with preservative (metal housing only). 
• Spray preservative on intake and exhaust side of exhaust turbocharger (where applicable) and reconnect the lines. 
• Remove valve cover and spray inside of valve cover, valve stems, springs, rocker, etc. with preservative oil. 
• Remove injection nozzle and coat cylinder surface with preservative oil. Hold stop lever in zero delivery position 
and crank engine manually several times. Refit injection nozzles with new seals (at an operation hour of min. 100

• Spray radiator cover and tank cover or radiator cover on expansion tank lightly with preservative oil and refit. 
• Close off intake and exhaust apertures (e.g.  with tape or end caps). 
Note: For storage for more than 12 months, the preservation 
measures shall be checked annually and supplemented 
as necessary.

Seite/Page 118   -   Kapitel/Chapter 15: General operation 29.4.25
15.2 Starting preparation / Checks (daily)
15.2.1 Marine version
1. Oil level control (ideal level: 2/3 MAX).
The level should be about 2/3 of the maximum level of a cold engine. 
Further, if installed, the oil level of the oil-cooled bearing must be controlled before each start - see sediment bowl
at generator front cover!.
2. State of cooling water.
The external expansion tank should be filled up to 1/3 of the maximum in a cold state. It is very important that a
large expansion area remains above the cooling water level.
3. Check if sea cock for cooling water intake is open.
For safety reasons, the sea cock must be closed after the generator has been switched off. It should be re-
opened before starting the generator.
4. Check raw water filter. 
The raw water filter must be regularly checked and cleaned. The impeller fatigue increases, if residual affects the
raw water intake.
5. Visual inspection.
Control fixing bolts, check hose connectors for leakages, control electrical connections.
6. Switch off the load.
The generator should only be started without load.
7. Open fuel valve, if installed.

15.5 iControl2-Emergency-Stop
The iControl2 is prepared for the connection of an emergency stop. The socket for the emergency stop is in the
cable harness integrated and bridged (1X1, optional emergency off). The bridge must be removed and the
emergency stop connected.
After the emergency stop is initiated, the servo drives to zero position, all out of the iControl controller are switched
off and the power supply to the inverter is switched off. 
The Panel shows „Emergency Stop“.

excerpts.text:
Keep PEAK LOADING demand in mind when switching on electrical devices (esp. fridge compressors, electric
motors, battery chargers, kettles, etc.) which are fed by the generator. Careful „powering up“ (gradual loading) of the
electrical demand on the generator will help prolong the life of your genset! The genset can be run for several hours
at partial load (i.e. 2/3 of rated power), however it is not advised that it is run for more than 2-3 hours at full load. The
Panda is designed so as not to overheat even under extreme conditions. Note: The exhaust gas will become sooty
during peak-load operation.
Effects of Short Circuiting and Overloading on the Generator
The generator cannot be damaged by short circuiting or overloading. Short circuiting and overloading suppress the
magnetic excitation of the generator, thus, no current is generated and the voltage will collapse. This condition is
immediately offset once the short-circuit has been eliminated and/or the electrical overload removed.

20.8 Operation manual
20.8.1 Primary remarks / Winter operation
The PMGi can operate in the range of -20 °C to +40 °C.
20.8.2 Load at the PMGi
Do not overload the PMGi. It will go on error.
20.8.3 Automatic start
The generator can start (depending on the remote control panel) by an external signal (automatic start)
If you use this option make sure that the load is connected to the PMGi after the output has reached the nominal
230 V / 50 Hz and not to overload the PMGi (some electronic devices, such like air conditions, need an higher start
current). May use a relay which connect the load at the nominal voltage (f.e.120 V or 230 V.).
20.9 Status LEDs
20.10Cooling of the PMGi
Inside of the PMGi a fan is mounted.
Do not cover the air holes and grille.
The heat sink and the fan of the PMGi may become dirty as a consequence of the use of the generator, and so the
unit can loose a part of their heat transfer characteristic. Every 6 months it is necessary to visual inspect the heat
sinks and clean it with compressed air. At every Generator service the fan of the PMGi should be cleaned by the
special trained person.

excerpts.text:
The area around batteries is a potentially explosive 
atmosphere.
• Ensure that no tools are dropped on the battery terminals; cover them as necessary.
• Do not wear jewellery or watches on your arms during installation that might short-circuit the battery. 
Otherwise, there is a risk of skin burns.
• Protect all battery contacts against accidental contact.
• For battery banks: Use only deep cycle batteries. Starter batteries are not suitable. Lead-acid gel 
batteries are recommended. They are maintenance-free, cycle stable, and do not release gases.
• Never charge a frozen battery.
• Avoid battery short-circuits.
• Ensure proper ventilation of the battery to vent gases that may be released.
• Battery connection terminals must be checked for proper seating before operation.
• Battery connection cables shall be installed with utmost care and shall be checked for excessive heating 
under load. Check the battery near vibrating components regularly for chafing and insulation defects. 
ATTENTION! For battery charger generators (Fischer Panda AGT-DC)! 
Prior to installation, verify that the voltage of the battery bank complies with the output voltage of
the generator.

excerpts.text:
29.4.25 Kapitel/Chapter 16: Installation   -   Seite/Page 141
16.5 Master and Slave Panels
With the iControl2 it is possible to have up to four remote control panels at one iGenerator. (One Master + three
Slave)
The standard iControl panel has the Art. No. 21.02.02.131P. This Panel has integrated termination resistors and is
the Master Panel.
The iControl2 Slave Panel has the Art. No. 21.02.02.132P. It is marked with a sticker „Slave Panel“ at the back side.
In a iControl system with Master and Slave panels, The Master must be the last one in the row, so that the
termination resistor is at the end of the FP-BUS.
The Slave Panel can not be used alone. The Slave Panels must be connected between the iControl controller (at the
iGenerator) and the Master Panel. 
The Master Slave configuration can be used at iGenerators with software 2.3 at Controller and Panel.
All Panels (Master and Slave) has the Address „1“ as standard. The Address can be changed in the menu. Possible
are 1, 2, 3 and 4. Each Panel must have a unique address.
To use the automatic start option, the connection of the automatic start must be at the panel with the address „1“.

20.7.1.1  Uninterrupted AC power (UPS function)
Due to the fact that the power inverter connects the shore power immediately to the domestic grid (to fast), the PMGi
gets overloaded and shut down with an error.
UPS Function must be deactivated.
20.7.1.2  Dynamic current limiter 
With inductive load the dynamic current limiter will raise up the Voltage in the DC circuit. These over voltage can

20.7.3 Victron AC out

17. Maintenance
17.1 Maintenance of the iControl2 controller
The iControl2 controller is maintenance-free. The fuses of the controller are self-healing.
17.1.1 Cleaning the iControl2 controller
The housing shall be cleaned within the scope of the overall generator cleaning. The housing can be wiped off with
a soft, lightly dampened cloth. In doing so, it must be ensured that no moisture enters the jacks and the housing.
17.2 Maintenance of the iControl2 remote control panel
The iControl2 remote control panel is maintenance-free.
17.2.1 Cleaning the iControl2 controller
The display can be cleaned with a soft cloth dampened lightly with soapy water. Harsh cleaning agents are not
suitable and can cause the display film to turn dull.

assembled_user_prompt:
You extract an interaction profile for one piece of marine equipment from excerpts
of its official manual(s). You record observable facts about how humans interact
with the device. You do NOT judge importance or guide tier. You do NOT write guest
prose.

Rules:

1. Use ONLY the manual text provided. No general knowledge about this product or
   brand. Unmentioned = empty array / false / "unclear". Never guess.
   device.category_freeform must be the manual's own product category words
   (e.g. "charger/inverter", "MPPT solar charger") — never registry/taxonomy
   tokens such as electrical_dc, propulsion, freshwater_system.

2. Empty is meaningful. A busbar has no control surfaces and no operator actions;
   emit empty arrays.

3. audience per action, by the manual's own framing:
   - routine steps addressed to "you" → operator
   - "consult your dealer", "qualified personnel", installation-chapter procedures,
     DIP-switch / DIP table configuration → installer_or_technician
     (DIP configuration is always installer_or_technician + commissioning)
   - genuinely both → either

4. context per action: daily / situational / emergency / commissioning /
   maintenance. One procedure fitting two contexts → two entries.

4b. occasion per action (optional): when/why the operator performs the action,
   paraphrased from THIS text only (trigger, purpose, "if …", "to avoid …").
   Leave empty / omit when the manual does not state when or why. Never invent
   vessel SOP (e.g. "every new dock") not in THIS text. Purpose-only restatements
   of the verb ("to set the limit") are not occasions.

5. Split actions: each action string is one verb phrase with one context.
   "monitor and configure via the app" → two actions (e.g. monitor → daily;
   configure → situational or commissioning per the manual's framing).
   Splitting bundled actions must never remove actions. Every distinct
   procedure the manual addresses to the operator must appear, including
   shutdown, restart, and fault/error-code procedures.
   has_emergency_procedure: true when the manual has an error-code / fault
   table or protective-disconnect recovery — not merely because an orderly
   service/storage shutdown exists (see calibration J).

6. control_surfaces on the base product vs. accessories sold separately: mark the
   latter optional_accessory: true. If a capability requires another product, also
   add a requires_devices entry with needed_for pointing at that field path
   (e.g. "control_surfaces[0]" or "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network").
   Never set needed_for to "" / null. Never attach requires_devices to a surface
   with optional_accessory: false except software_app (downloadable app for a
   built-in surface — record it with requirement_kind: software_app).
   Multiple requires_devices entries that share the same needed_for are OR
   alternatives (any one present on the vessel activates the capability);
   emit separate entries per alternative product — never stuff "A or B" into
   one description_verbatim when both are first-class options.
   requirement_kind (required on each entry):
     device — vessel line-item product (GX device, remote panel, safety relay)
     cable_or_consumable — cable/cord/dongle/consumable (VE.Direct cable)
     software_app — downloadable app (VictronConnect app)
     commissioning_tool — installer config/commissioning software
       (MasterAdjust, CZone Configuration Tool)

7. networks: record names verbatim in speaks (with physical_or_wireless when known).
   Ports/protocols that exist on the device belong in speaks even when their
   usefulness depends on another product — express that dependency with
   requires_devices, not by omitting the speak or zeroing the capability.
   Speak / bridge names must appear verbatim (or as clear tokens) in THESE
   excerpts — never import bus names from calibration examples (MasterBus,
   VE.Direct, etc.). Record a bridge ONLY if the manual explicitly describes
   translating/gatewaying between two named networks. Being connectable to a
   network is speaks, not bridges.

7b. data_roles — subject is ALWAYS the device under extraction:
   - exposes_data_to_network: this device publishes its own data onto a network
   - displays_data_from_other_devices: this device shows data originating
     elsewhere
   - controllable_from_network: THIS device's own functions can be commanded by
     another node/app over a network
   Anti-pattern: controlling other devices (CZone circuits/loads, radar
   standby, connected gear) is NOT controllable_from_network — record those as
   operator_actions / displays_data / speaks as appropriate. Do not attach
   evidence notes like "control devices via the CZone network" to
   controllable_from_network.

8. Multiple model variants in one manual: profile the requested model only. Ignore
   accessories, requirements, operator_actions, ui_pages, and procedures that the
   manual scopes only to other variants (e.g. "applies to NSO 4 and Zeus SRX only"
   when extracting Zeus SR — SR ≠ SRX). Do not omit shared content that applies to
   the requested model. If the manual does not distinguish, say so in
   confidence.notes and lower confidence.overall. Procedure-inventory reconcile
   classifies sibling-only items as not_applicable:other_variant (not an
   extraction miss).

9. evidence: 1-8 objects {supports_field, manual_section, note}. supports_field is
   the schema path justified. Prefer operator_actions[action=<exact action text>]
   over brittle operator_actions[N] indices. manual_section is a short section
   title/heading only (verbatim heading words — never a sentence, never letter
   crumbs like "D E"). Prefer each excerpt's source_heading_guess when present.
   Never take headings from diagram/figure callout labels (lone letters or
   letter-runs such as "A B C" / "D E", or "I Alerts Select…" caption keys) —
   those are figure markers, not section titles. When a supporting excerpt
   opens mid-figure, use the carried section title (source_heading_guess from
   the preceding page), not the first line of the chunk. note is a <=12-word
   paraphrase — never copy manual sentences. Prefer coverage across
   consequential fields — not multiple evidence rows for one field. Fill in
   this priority order (stop when the cap is reached):
   (a) data_roles fields set true, and each requires_devices entry;
   (b) safety_role fields set true;
   (c) representative operator_actions (at least the emergency-context ones);
   (d) control_surfaces.
   Do not invent extra mandatory evidence beyond what this priority covers.

10. Optional protected_by / protects / supply_requirements: record when the
    manual explicitly states guard devices or supply needs — including in
    installer-framed installation chapters. audience applies to
    operator_actions only, never to requirement fields. Otherwise empty arrays.

11. safety_role keys are exactly: is_protective_device, has_manual_override,
    has_emergency_procedure. Do not invent other keys.

12. Platforms (shared software UI such as CZone 2.0): set entity_kind:
    "platform" and documented_version from the cover / update section
    (e.g. "CZone 2.0 v1.1 (software v6.12.4.0+)"). Emit ui_pages[] — one
    entry per upper-level app page named in the intro diagram or page
    chapters. Each ui_page has name, purpose, appears_if_gate, and actions.
    appears_if_gate: when the manual says the page appears only if equipment
    is configured, set verbatim to the full gate sentence,
    description_verbatim to the equipment noun phrase, and functional_class
    to a short token (acmi | mastervolt_inverter_charger | supported_hvac).
    When the page is always present, set all three gate fields to "".
    Emit alarm_severity[] when severity colours are listed
    (level_verbatim + color_verbatim).
    Physical devices with an on-device screen (MFDs, chargers with menus):
    entity_kind "device"; one control_surfaces row per physical screen
    (typically one touchscreen); named menus / panels / tabs / drawers go in
    ui_pages[], never as extra touchscreen surfaces. Omit empty settings /
    setup pages with no in-page actions. Ordinary devices with no software UI:
    documented_version "", ui_pages [], alarm_severity [].

CALIBRATION — these examples concern OTHER devices / situations. Match the
field decisions (context, audience, optional_accessory, dependency shape).
Do NOT copy action strings or product-specific wording from the examples into
the device under extraction unless that wording appears in THIS device's
manual excerpts.

A. "To connect shore power: set the AC input current limit to match the dock
   supply using the rotary selector."
   → context: situational; audience: operator; one verb-phrase action naming
     the current-limit adjustment (word it from the manual under extraction).

B. "Battery type selection must be performed at commissioning. Consult your
   Balmar dealer for programming assistance."
   → context: commissioning; audience: installer_or_technician; one action for
     the battery-type selection (word it from the manual under extraction).

C. "If the BMS has disconnected the battery, remove the fault cause, then press
   and hold the reset button for 5 seconds."
   → context: emergency; audience: operator; one action describing that device's
     protective-disconnect recovery in the manual's own words (never paste a
     stock phrase from these examples).

D. "The charger can be monitored and configured via the VictronConnect app over
   Bluetooth."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, location_class: wireless,
     optional_accessory: false;
     PLUS two operator_actions: "monitor charger via VictronConnect app"
     (daily) and "configure charger via VictronConnect app" (situational or
     commissioning per framing) — never one combined "monitor and configure" action.

E. "With the optional Bluetooth Gateway, settings can be adjusted from the
   smartphone app."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, optional_accessory: true;
     plus requires_devices entry for the gateway with
     needed_for: "control_surfaces[0]".

F. "The unit connects to the NMEA 2000 backbone and transmits battery status PGNs."
   → networks.speaks: "NMEA 2000"; data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     bridges: EMPTY.

G. "The interface links the MasterBus network to CZone, allowing CZone displays
   to show MasterBus device data."
   → networks.bridges: [{from: "MasterBus", to: "CZone"}].

H. "Connect the unit's VE.Direct port to a GX device to monitor it remotely via VRM."
   → networks.speaks includes "VE.Direct" (wired);
     data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "GX device",
     needed_for: "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network"}.
   Rationale: the port exists on the device → speaks; usefulness depending on
   another product → requires_devices, not by zeroing the capability or omitting
   the speak.

I. "BlueSolar models without built-in Bluetooth require the VE.Direct Bluetooth
   Smart Dongle." (when profiling a SmartSolar with built-in Bluetooth)
   → NO requires_devices entry for the dongle and NO control_surface change.
   Accessories that apply only to other model variants are ignored (see rule 8).

J. "To shut down the charger for service or storage: disconnect the PV supply,
   then the battery supply."
   → action: "shutdown the solar charger" (or equivalent); context: situational
   (or maintenance), NOT emergency. Prescribed orderly shutdown for service/
   storage is not an emergency procedure; fault response (error codes,
   protective disconnect recovery) is.

K. "The unit can be operated with the optional MasterView remote panel, installed
   at a convenient location."
   → control_surface: {surface: remote_panel_accessory, location_class:
     remote_wired, optional_accessory: true, label_verbatim from the manual};
     PLUS requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "MasterView remote panel"
     (or the manual's own panel name), needed_for: that surface's path}.
   Rationale: a separately sold panel/display that operates THIS device is a
   control surface of this device, not a different product.

L. "Install a fuse in the positive DC supply cable within 30cm of the battery."
